name: Mianserin improved aged-mouse healthspan and lifespan
summary: Mianserin adds a preclinical senomorphic signal by linking calcium homeostasis, SASP signaling, human-derived cell senescence, and mouse healthspan/lifespan.
statement: Mianserin attenuated senescence in cells derived from Hutchinson-Gilford progeria syndrome patients and naturally aging humans, improved aging phenotypes, and extended lifespan in LmnaG609G/G609G progeroid and naturally aged mice.
population_or_model: Human aging/progeria-derived cell systems, progeroid mice, and naturally aged mice
time_horizon: Preclinical cell and mouse experiments reported in 2026
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract reports significant lifespan extension in progeroid and naturally aged mice but does not provide a single effect size in the abstract.
caveats: No treated-human aging trial was reported.
The source combines human-derived cell systems, progeroid mice, and naturally aged mice, so it does not establish human benefit.
The preventive-aging dose, schedule, tissue selection, and safety balance for mianserin remain unresolved.
